description: Pure-Python handler package `esr_handler_feishu_app` for actor_type=feishu_app_proxy. Receives feishu adapter msg_received events from the Elixir runtime, maintains a frozen pydantic FeishuAppState, and returns (new_state, [Action]) — routing chat messages to bound threads, instantiating new thread sub-topologies via InvokeCommand, or dropping non-msg events. PRD 05 F06/F07/F08/F09, spec v0.2 §3.3.

key_symbols.name: manifest
key_symbols.description: ESR handler manifest (PRD 05 F02): name=feishu_app, version=0.1.0, module=esr_handler_feishu_app.on_msg, entry=on_msg, actor_type=feishu_app_proxy, allowed_imports=[].

key_symbols.name: project
key_symbols.description: esr-handler-feishu-app v0.1.0, requires-python>=3.11, depends on esr (editable path ../../py). pytest asyncio_mode=auto, --import-mode=importlib.

key_symbols.name: __version__
key_symbols.description: Package version '0.1.0'.

key_symbols.name: FeishuAppState
key_symbols.description: Frozen pydantic BaseModel decorated with @handler_state(actor_type='feishu_app_proxy', schema_version=1). model_config={'frozen': True}. Fields: app_id:str='', bound_threads:frozenset[str]=frozenset(), active_thread_by_chat:dict[str,str]={}, last_chat_id:str=''.

key_symbols.name: FeishuAppState.with_added_thread
key_symbols.description: Return new state with thread_id added to bound_threads (set union via model_copy).

key_symbols.name: FeishuAppState.with_active_thread
key_symbols.description: Return new state with active_thread_by_chat[chat_id]=thread_id (copy-then-update).

key_symbols.name: FeishuAppState.with_last_chat_id
key_symbols.description: Return new state with last_chat_id replaced.

key_symbols.name: _NEW_SESSION_PREFIX
key_symbols.description: '/new-session ' — v0.2 slash-command prefix.

key_symbols.name: _NEW_THREAD_PREFIX
key_symbols.description: '/new-thread ' — v0.1 backward-compat alias (removed in P9).

key_symbols.name: _AT_PREFIX
key_symbols.description: '@' — at-address routing prefix.

key_symbols.name: on_msg
key_symbols.description: @handler(actor_type='feishu_app_proxy', name='on_msg'). Signature (state: FeishuAppState, event: Event) -> (FeishuAppState, list[Action]). Ignores non msg_received events; captures last_chat_id on first inbound; dispatches to /new-session, /new-thread (legacy), @-routed, or active_thread_by_chat fallback.

key_symbols.name: _handle_new_session
key_symbols.description: Parse '/new-session <workspace> tag=<tag>' (v0.2) or '/new-thread <tag>' (v0.1 legacy, default_workspace='legacy'). Default tag = slug of message_id[:12] if absent. Returns state.with_added_thread(tag).with_active_thread(chat_id, tag) and emits InvokeCommand(name='feishu-thread-session', params={thread_id, chat_id, workspace, tag}).

key_symbols.name: _handle_at_routed
key_symbols.description: Parse '@<tag> <body>' — if tag in bound_threads, emit Route(target=f'thread:{tag}', msg={event_type:'msg_received', args: event.args with content=body}). Unknown tag → no-op.

key_symbols.name: test_state_has_defaults
key_symbols.description: FeishuAppState() has app_id='' and bound_threads=frozenset().

key_symbols.name: test_state_equality_by_value
key_symbols.description: Two states with identical fields compare equal.

key_symbols.name: test_state_is_frozen
key_symbols.description: Direct attribute assignment raises pydantic.ValidationError (frozen=True).

key_symbols.name: test_with_added_thread_returns_new_state
key_symbols.description: with_added_thread is immutable and fluent/stackable.

key_symbols.name: test_new_session_triggers_invoke_command
key_symbols.description: '/new-session esr-dev tag=dev-root' → bound_threads={dev-root}, active_thread_by_chat={oc_abc:dev-root}, InvokeCommand('feishu-thread-session', {thread_id, chat_id, workspace, tag}).

key_symbols.name: test_new_thread_legacy_alias_still_works
key_symbols.description: '/new-thread alpha' → InvokeCommand with workspace='legacy', thread_id='alpha'.

key_symbols.name: test_at_prefix_addressing_routes_to_named_thread
key_symbols.description: '@beta hello there' with beta in bound_threads → Route(target='thread:beta', content='hello there').

key_symbols.name: test_non_command_msg_routes_to_active_thread_by_chat
key_symbols.description: Plain text falls back to active_thread_by_chat lookup → Route(target='thread:alpha').

key_symbols.name: test_reaction_event_ignored
key_symbols.description: event_type='reaction_added' → (state, []); state identity preserved.

key_symbols.name: test_arbitrary_event_ignored
key_symbols.description: Unknown event_type → no-op.

key_symbols.name: test_state_tracks_active_thread_per_chat
key_symbols.description: with_active_thread is immutable per-chat map update.

key_symbols.name: test_state_tracks_last_chat_id
key_symbols.description: with_last_chat_id sets last_chat_id.

interfaces.name: FeishuAppState (handler state model)
interfaces.description: Frozen pydantic BaseModel registered via @handler_state(actor_type='feishu_app_proxy', schema_version=1). Persisted per-actor state; fluent with_* methods return new instances.

interfaces.name: on_msg (handler entrypoint)
interfaces.description: @handler(actor_type='feishu_app_proxy', name='on_msg'). Pure function (FeishuAppState, Event) -> (FeishuAppState, list[Action]). Returned actions are Route (to thread:<tag>) or InvokeCommand (feishu-thread-session) — Emit is NOT used in this handler.

dependencies: esr (editable path to py/) — imports Action, Event, InvokeCommand, Route, handler, handler_state
pydantic (BaseModel with model_config frozen=True) — provided transitively via esr
stdlib only (no I/O, no network, no filesystem, no os, no subprocess, no time, no random)

user_flows.name: User starts a new thread session via /new-session
steps: Feishu user sends '/new-session <workspace> tag=<tag>' in a chat (or legacy '/new-thread <tag>').
Feishu adapter forwards msg_received event (args: content, chat_id, message_id) to the feishu_app_proxy actor.
on_msg captures last_chat_id if new, parses the prefix, derives tag (default = slug of message_id[:12]).
Returns new state with tag added to bound_threads and registered as the chat's active thread.
Emits InvokeCommand(name='feishu-thread-session', params={thread_id, chat_id, workspace, tag}) — Elixir Instantiator validates workspace.app_id and spawns the thread sub-topology (v0.2 §3.3).

user_flows.name: User addresses a specific thread via @<tag>
steps: on_msg strips the '@' prefix, splits at first space.
If tag is in bound_threads, emits Route(target='thread:<tag>', msg={event_type:'msg_received', args with content=body}).

user_flows.name: Plain chat message routed to the active thread
steps: User sends a message without a prefix.
If the thread_id exists in bound_threads, emits Route(target='thread:<thread_id>', msg=event copy).

user_flows.name: Non-msg events are dropped
steps: Events with event_type != 'msg_received' (e.g. reaction_added, card_action) return (state, []) with identity-preserved state.

notes: PURITY: verified — on_msg.py imports only `from __future__ import annotations`, `from esr import Action, Event, InvokeCommand, Route, handler` and its own state module; state.py imports only pydantic.BaseModel + esr.handler_state. No os/io/subprocess/socket/time/random/requests imports; no side effects; all state mutations go through pydantic model_copy (frozen=True enforced). manifest declares allowed_imports=[] consistent with the empty import surface. ACTION SHAPES EMITTED: (1) InvokeCommand('feishu-thread-session', {thread_id, chat_id, workspace, tag}) for /new-session and legacy /new-thread; (2) Route(target='thread:<tag>', msg={event_type, args}) for @-addressed and fallback routes; (3) Emit is NEVER returned by this handler — outbound feishu messages are the Elixir/thread handler's responsibility. EVENT TYPES: only msg_received is acted on; all other event_types return (state, []) unchanged. STATE SEMANTICS: app_id (reserved, currently unused by on_msg — set at instantiation time by Elixir); bound_threads (all thread tags ever spawned in this chat scope); active_thread_by_chat (per chat_id → latest thread_id for fallback routing); last_chat_id (captured on first inbound — consumed by the /new-session default-tag branch and potentially by later out-of-band flows). V0.2 QUIRK: workspace app_id validation is done in the Elixir Instantiator (P6-3) because the pure-Python sandbox cannot see AdapterHub.Registry; unregistered workspace → {:error, :app_not_registered} surfaced back through the feishu_app Route-to-error path (TODO v0.3). LEGACY: /new-thread alias uses default_workspace='legacy' and takes the first token as the tag — will be removed in P9 together with the scenario YAML rewrite.
